page: Microsoft Azure EC8es_v6 is an x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the ECEV6 server family. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel processor and runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The instance is configured with 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high resource density of 8.0 GB of RAM per vCPU.

This server does not include any local storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met via external block storage. It is designated as Premium Storage capable, allowing integration with high-performance external storage volumes. There are no bundled GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses included with this instance.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the EC8es_v6 provides a high memory-to-vCPU ratio, making it a viable option for memory-intensive workloads that do not require local scratch space. This configuration is suitable for workloads such as relational databases, in-memory caching, and data analytics pipelines that benefit from large memory footprints relative to compute capacity.
description: Microsoft Azure EC8es_v6 is an x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine from the ECEV6 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel processor and is equipped with 64.0 GB of RAM, providing a memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. The instance does not include local storage but is Premium Storage capable, allowing connection to high-performance external storage. With zero bundled GPUs and no complimentary public IPv4 addresses, this server is designed for memory-intensive workloads. The high memory-to-vCPU ratio makes it qualitatively cost-efficient for applications requiring large memory footprints without the overhead of local storage. It is suitable for database hosting, in-memory caching, and enterprise applications.
